chapters.chapter_summary: The patient was positioned supine with a shoulder roll elevating the right shoulder and head turned to the left, exposing the temporal parietal area. General anesthesia was administered.
A circular bone flap was elevated using a single burr hole, exposing the underlying relaxed dura. The dura was then opened, revealing the tumor.
The tumor was carefully separated from the surrounding brain tissue using surgical instruments. The tumor was gradually detached and lifted out of the brain.
The tumor was removed in a gross total fashion. Sodium fluorescein-guided microscopy was used to identify areas suspicious for residual tumor, which were further explored and removed.

Hemostasis was achieved by applying pressure and using tools to control bleeding. The cavity was covered with a hydrogen peroxide-soaked cotton ball for both destruction of neoplastic cells and hemostasis.
The dura was re-approximated and duroplasty was completed. The bone flap was re-approximated, and the border between the bone flap and the cranium was covered with a synthetic agent.
The patient had no neurological deficit and was discharged home. Post-operative MRI scans showed no radiographic evidence of residual or recurrent tumor.

operative_note.title: Temporal Parietal Craniotomy and Tumor Resection

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a temporal parietal craniotomy and tumor resection on a 20-plus year old patient who presented with several weeks history of headache, progressive weakness on the left side of the body, and difficulty with gait and balance.
SOAP.Objective: I identified a large mass at the temporo-parietal junction with significant local mass effect and vasogenic edema. I observed the tumor being carefully separated from the surrounding brain tissue using surgical instruments. Sodium fluorescein-guided microscopy was used to confirm the location of the tumor and identify areas suspicious for residual tumor.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful with gross total resection of the tumor. No injury to the surrounding brain tissue was observed. Post-operative MRI scans showed no radiographic evidence of residual or recurrent tumor.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the wound in layers and covered the sutured incision line with synthetic skin graft material. The patient had no neurological deficit and was discharged home. Follow-up MRI scans were planned to monitor for any recurrence.
